# Socheli Pro Editor ‚Äî DaVinci-Level Roadmap (Build Bible)

*One editorial brain that grades, cuts, mixes, and composites toward measured targets on our own Remotion + ffmpeg stack ‚Äî no DaVinci dependency.*

---

## 1. Vision

A **DaVinci-level AI editor** on Socheli is not a feature list ‚Äî it is one evidence-grounded agent that occupies four senior-craft roles and drives each to a *judged target*, then learns from what performs:

- **Colorist** ‚Äî balances exposure, neutralizes or stylizes white balance, and holds tone consistent scene-to-scene, grading *with* real ffmpeg scopes (waveform / vectorscope / histogram) read off rendered frames, not by prose alone.
- **Editor** ‚Äî cuts on a real multi-track timeline with ripple / roll / slip / slide trims, razor cuts, markers, and the signature move the current flat `Scene[]` model literally cannot express: **J/L cuts** that decouple audio from picture.
- **Mixer** ‚Äî runs a Fairlight-grade desk: keyframed gain/pan automation, a per-track EQ/comp/de-ess/de-noise chain, word-aware sidechain ducking, and a -14 LUFS master ‚Äî judged by real meters (integrated/short-term LUFS, true-peak, LRA).
- **Compositor** ‚Äî wires a Fusion-style node graph (mask ‚Üí key ‚Üí glow ‚Üí blend ‚Üí displace ‚Üí tracker-attach) per scene, authored from frame perception, with an ffmpeg pre-bake escape hatch for true keys/tracking SVG can't reach.

The thread tying all four together already exists and is *proven on one axis*: `creative/edl.ts` `applyEdlToStoryboard` (THE BRIDGE, edl.ts:273) deterministically projects free-text editorial intent ‚Üí schema-clamped render params, **skips locked scenes, clamps every numeric, skips-not-throws** on one bad decision. `creative/passes.ts` runs 8 specialist passes (PASS_ORDER at passes.ts:44), each with a field whitelist (PASS_FIELDS:62) and OBSERVED evidence from `creative/signals.ts` (`editSignals`, `signalsSummary` at signals.ts:180) which reads `editor_analyze_av`'s ffmpeg diagnostics (`videoDiagnostics` at editor-tools.ts:246). `review.ts` scores, `taste.ts`/`perf.ts` learn.

**The vision is to take that one-axis loop ‚Äî perceive ‚Üí propose intent ‚Üí bridge (clamped, locked-safe) ‚Üí review ‚Üí learn ‚Äî and replicate it across all four crafts, each grounded in real measured pixels and meters, each with a real render target the bridge can write to.** Today three of the four crafts have *no real render target*: there is no per-scene grade, no timeline, and no audio chain. This roadmap builds those targets, then points the existing loop at them.

---

## 2. Architecture spine

### 2.1 The unified data model ‚Äî EDL vs ProjectTimeline (decision)

Three of the five designs independently proposed promoting the Edl to a richer master (ProjectTimeline / Timeline). **They are describing the same need from different angles, and they must be reconciled into ONE model or the four subsystems fragment into four incompatible param paths ‚Äî the single largest risk in this program.**

**Decision (opinionated):**

> **The Edl stays the editorial INTENT layer (free-text per-beat: `colorIntent` / `mixIntent` / `motionIntent` / `visualIntent` / `fn` / `keep` / `pacingSec` / `emphasis`). The `Storyboard` stays the on-disk render source of truth. The `Timeline` (NLE) becomes the trim-precise REALIZATION layer, and `compileTimeline` projects it back onto `storyboard` + `mix`. There is NO separate "ProjectTimeline" record ‚Äî the "ProjectTimeline" concept from the Color and Foundation designs is satisfied by `timeline_get`'s derived read-only view, not a fourth persisted layer.**

Rationale: the Foundation design's own risk note nails it ‚Äî *"Promoting EDL to ProjectTimeline risks a second source of truth ‚Äî enforce v1 as a computed VIEW or the four subsystems fragment exactly as feared."* We honor that by making the timeline the only new persisted editorial layer, and a **computed timeline view** (frame-addressed item table) the single inspection surface all four crafts read.

**Canonical order of operations (sacred, enforced):**

```
passes edit the Edl
   ‚Üí applyEdlToStoryboard  (intent ‚Üí storyboard.scenes[].style{grade,comp}, .durationSec, + item.mix)
   ‚Üí timeline_build         (seed/refresh item.timeline: one A-clip per visible scene + audio clips from Mix)
   ‚Üí manual NLE trims        (timeline_trim / razor / jl_cut ‚Äî edit item.timeline)
   ‚Üí compileTimeline         (timeline ‚Üí storyboard + mix, clamped, locked-safe)
   ‚Üí render
```

**Precedence rule (resolves the worst desync risk):** once `item.timeline.compiledAt` is set, **the timeline owns timing** ‚Äî `clip.durationSec` wins over `EdlDecision.pacingSec`. After first `timeline_build`, the Edl becomes *intent-only for non-timing axes* (grade / mix / compositing / transition), and stops driving durations. `timeline_build` is **idempotent and edit-preserving**: re-seeding preserves manual trims where a `sceneRef` still matches.

### 2.2 Where each subsystem writes (one bridge surface, four compilers)

`applyEdlToStoryboard` generalizes from one bridge into a family of deterministic compilers that **all share its exact contract** (clamp every numeric ‚Üí schema band; never touch a `locked` scene; skip-not-throw; leave the cut renderable):

| Craft | Intent field | Compiler | Render target (NEW) |
|---|---|---|---|
| Colorist | `colorIntent` (+structured `grade`) | `applyColorAndType` ‚Üí `colorIntentToGrade` | `scene.style.grade` + `storyboard.grade` |
| Mixer | `mixIntent` | `parseMixIntent` (enriched) | `mix.tracks[].{eq,comp,...}` + `mix.clips[]` automation |
| Editor | `pacingSec`/`keep`/`transitionIn` | (existing) + `timeline_build`/trims | `item.timeline` ‚Üí `compileTimeline` |
| Compositor | `visualIntent` + `colorIntent` | `buildCompFromIntents` | `scene.style.comp` (EffectGraph) + `storyboard.comp` |

Every compiler is a sibling of `applyColorAndType` (edl.ts:482) / `applyMotionIntent` (edl.ts:451) / `parseMixIntent` (edl.ts:537), reusing the same `clamp`/`round2`/locked-skip discipline already in the file.

### 2.3 How the creative layer extends to drive all four

The pass model **is already the senior-editor judgment loop** and extends cleanly to four discipline tracks mapped onto the existing PASS_ORDER (picture ‚Üí sound ‚Üí text ‚Üí color ‚Üí QA):

- **EDITOR track** = `assembly` + `pacing` + `emotion` passes ‚Üí `keep`/`pacingSec`/`transitionIn` + (new) TimelineOps. Evidence: read/speak budgets + scene-change/freeze/silence detect (`editSignals` already computes `recommendMinSec`, `deadAir` at signals.ts:34).
- **MIXER track** = `audio` pass ‚Üí `mixIntent` ‚Üí AudioGraph. Evidence: `volumedetect`/`silencedetect` **+ new** ebur128 LUFS/TP/LRA + per-region RMS.
- **COLORIST track** = `color` pass ‚Üí `colorIntent`/structured grade. Evidence: **new** `editor_color_scopes` (waveform/vectorscope/histogram + `signalstats` numbers).
- **COMPOSITOR track** = `visual` pass ‚Üí `visualIntent` ‚Üí EffectGraph. Evidence: `editor_video_evidence` (dense frames + pixel metrics + motion deltas + OCR, editor-tools.ts:~1996) ‚Äî *already exists*.

`signals.ts` `gatherEvidence` (signals.ts:94) gains a `scope` block (colorist) and a `meter` block (mixer); `signalsSummary` (signals.ts:180) prints per-scene exposure/WB/sat and LUFS tables the pass prompts read. `review.ts` `ReviewScores` already has `audioClarity`/`technicalPolish` axes ‚Äî each craft scores its own axis. `taste.ts`/`perf.ts` learn per-channel, per-discipline rules and feed performance back. **Same loop, four targets.**

---

## 3. The render-engine fix (TransitionSeries / React-19) ‚Äî M0, gates everything pixel

**The bug:** under React 19.2.3 + Remotion 4.0.461, `<TransitionSeries>` (Post.tsx:1049, built via `scenes.flatMap` with one `TransitionSeries.Sequence` + `TransitionSeries.Transition` per scene, Post.tsx:1052-1055) re-mounts a sequence **~16√ó** ‚Üí `device_mockup`/`diagram` scenes render stacked/black.

**Why it sequences FIRST (M0), ahead of all four subsystems:**

1. **It corrupts the colorist's evidence.** A scene that renders black/stacked reports **false clipping/crush** on `editor_color_scopes` ‚Äî the agent would grade against garbage. The Color design lists this as a hard dependency.
2. **It blocks compositing.** Per-scene comp graphs wrap scene content, so they'd be mounted ~16√ó too ‚Üí SVG-filter DOM blowup and the same stacked/black class of bug.
3. **It blocks true multi-track picture.** The NLE design's overlapping B-roll requires moving off `TransitionSeries` anyway.
4. **It does NOT block audio** (audio lives outside `TransitionSeries`) ‚Äî the only craft that can proceed in parallel.

**Fix path (primary):** replace the `TransitionSeries`/`scenes.flatMap` block with a **manual crossfade sequencer** ‚Äî each scene as `<Sequence from={startF} durationInFrames={durF+inTr}>` with the entry transition driven by interpolated opacity/transform over the first `trFs[i]` frames. The custom presentations already in Post.tsx (zoom/spin/glitch/scan/smash/terminal, ~786-898) become plain per-scene wrappers. This is also the natural home (`id='Timeline'` composition) for overlapping tracks later.

**Critical constraint:** `totalFrames()` (Post.tsx:127) and audio sync (duck envelope at Post.tsx:177, sfx `atF`, beat frames) are coupled to current per-scene frame offsets. **Keep frame offsets byte-identical and snapshot-test `totalFrames`** or captions/voice desync.

**Fallback (secondary, lower-leverage):** bump Remotion to a React-19.2.3-compat patch ‚Äî but that patch may not exist for 4.0.461; treat as a branch, not the path.

**Regression gate:** `render_verify_transitions` renders a DEBUG composition of `device_mockup`/`diagram` via `renderStill` and asserts each appears exactly once.

---

## 4. Per-subsystem designs

> **Schema note that de-risks everything below:** `sceneBase.style` is a plain `z.object({...}).optional()` (schemas/index.ts:160) ‚Äî **NOT `.strict()`** (verified). Two designs assumed `.strict()`. This means today's `sceneFilter` reading `style.brightness/contrast` (Post.tsx:452) *passes through* rather than being rejected, and adding optional `grade`/`comp` fields is low-risk and backward-compatible. The Foundation milestone "scene.style is .strict()" concern is **moot** ‚Äî but we still add fields as `.optional()` default-absent so legacy items validate unchanged.

### 4.1 Colorist ‚Äî real grade + ffmpeg scopes

**Target:** replace advisory-only `colorIntent` with a real, validated per-scene + global grade (lift/gamma/gain per channel, temp/tint, sat, contrast/pivot, curves, one optional HSL qualifier), applied through SVG `feComponentTransfer`/`feColorMatrix` layered into the existing `ColorGrade`. The AI grades *with* ffmpeg scopes in a render ‚Üí read-scopes ‚Üí grade-toward-targets loop.

**Current state:** `colorIntent` is ADVISORY ONLY ‚Äî `applyColorAndType` (edl.ts:482) maps it to `style.stroke`/`effectIntensity`/`shadow` proxies and stashes the literal prose in `scene.advisory.colorGrade` (edl.ts:508), never rendered. The comment at edl.ts states there is **no per-scene grade field**. `sceneFilter` (Post.tsx:452) emits only `brightness() contrast() saturate(1)` off un-validated `style.brightness/contrast`. The global `<ColorGrade>` (Post.tsx:1063, lib/grade.tsx) is gradient-overlay tone-split only ‚Äî no per-channel lift/gamma/gain, no temperature. `videoDiagnostics` (editor-tools.ts:246) runs `showwavespic` (audio waveform only), `volumedetect`, silence/freeze/black/scene detect ‚Äî **no video scopes**. `signals.ts` has no color signal. The `color` pass (passes.ts:51, 128) only sets `colorIntent` text.

**Data model (`@os/schemas`):**
- `ColorGrade` (new) on `sceneBase.style.grade`: `{lift,gamma,gain}` each `{r,g,b,master}` clamped, `temperature/tint` ¬±100, `saturation` 0‚Äì3, `contrast` 0‚Äì3, `pivot` ~0.435, `exposure` ¬±2, optional `curves`/`hsl` (max 2 qualifiers).
- `GlobalGrade` (new) on `storyboard.grade` ‚Äî same shape, composited *after* per-scene (project trim).
- `EdlDecision` += structured `grade: Partial<ColorGrade>` + `colorTargets` so the brain emits numbers, not just prose.
- `EvidenceSignal.scope` (signals.ts) += per-scene `{lumaP5/P50/P95, clipLow/High, rgbMean, wbBias, satMean, scopeImg}` + `consistency:{lumaSpread,wbSpread}`.
- `EditingTaste.colorTargets` += learned per-brand band.
- tokens `GradeParams` (moods.ts:7) += optional `lift/gamma/gain/temperature/saturation` + `gradeToColorGrade()` helper, so mood presets seed real primaries and `resolveStudio` (moods.ts:567) preserves every existing look.

**Render changes:**
- `lib/grade.tsx`: new `<GradePipeline grade={ColorGrade}/>` ‚Äî SVG `<filter>` with `feComponentTransfer` (per-channel `funcR/G/B`: lift=intercept, gain=slope, gamma=exponent) + 4√ó5 `feColorMatrix` for luma-weighted saturation (0.2126/0.7152/0.0722) + temp/tint bias + contrast-about-pivot + `type='table'` curves. `buildGradeFilterId(grade)` = stable rounded hash so each distinct grade defines one `<filter>` once (avoids per-frame remount flicker). Keep the gradient-overlay `ColorGrade` as the "look" layer on top.
- `Post.tsx`: `sceneFilter` emits `url(#grade_<hash>)`, falling back to legacy brightness/contrast when un-graded; mount `<GradePipeline>` defs in `SceneInner` (Post.tsx:716) keyed by grade hash. After the per-scene stack (Post.tsx:1063), wrap the visual stack in a global `<GradePipeline grade={storyboard.grade ?? studioGradeFromParams(studio.grade)}/>`.

**EDL bridge:** `applyColorAndType` stops being a stroke proxy ‚Üí writes real `scene.style.grade`. (1) structured `grade` from brain ‚Üí clamp + write directly. (2) else `colorIntentToGrade(text)` deterministic mapper: *"lift shadows"*‚Üí`lift.master+=0.06`; *"cool/teal"*‚Üí`temperature-=18`; *"crush blacks/punch"*‚Üí`contrast*=1.15,lift.master-=0.03`; *"desaturate"*‚Üí`saturation*=0.7`; *"teal-orange"*‚Üíhsl shadows‚Üíteal / highlights‚Üíorange; *"neutral"*‚Üísnap to 0. (3) legibility stroke/shadow demoted to secondary. (4) prose still recorded in `advisory.colorGrade`. **Global aggregation:** consistent intent across decisions ("teal-orange throughout") ‚Üí `storyboard.grade` carries the common trim, per-scene grades carry deltas ‚Äî consistency is structural, mirroring how `captionPreset`/`duckIntent` aggregate to `item.mix` today.

**AI pass (real, closed loop):** RENDER ‚Üí extract one mid-frame per scene (`rawFrame`/`sampleFrame`, editor-tools.ts:165-185) ‚Üí `editor_color_scopes` runs `signalstats`/waveform/vectorscope/histogram ‚Üí `ScopeSignal` ‚Üí `gatherEvidence` folds into `EvidenceSignal.scope` ‚Üí `signalsSummary` prints exposure/WB/sat table ‚Üí color pass reads table + `paletteIntent` + `EditingTaste.colorTargets`, emits structured grades toward **balanced exposure (P50 in band, minimal clipping), neutral-or-stylized WB, scene-to-scene consistency (penalize lumaSpread/wbSpread)** ‚Üí bridge writes `scene.style.grade` ‚Üí optional re-render + re-read to verify (convergence cap: prefer a single analytical pass solving gain/lift from measured P5/P50/P95 in closed form; cap re-render iterations at 2 ‚Äî M4 renders are expensive).

**New tools:** `editor_color_scopes` (editor-tools.ts), `creative_grade_scene`/`creative_grade_global` (creative-tools.ts), `creative_color_pass` (wraps the loop).

**Risks:** SVG `feComponentTransfer` interpolates in sRGB not linear-light (subtly off vs DaVinci ‚Äî mitigate by tuning mappers to scope *targets*, which is what the loop optimizes anyway); **scopes read a frame WITH grade + overlay/grain applied** ‚Äî define pre-look (grading) vs post-look (QA) scopes explicitly; the HSL qualifier secondary is approximate in pure SVG ‚Äî **defer it past v1**, ship lift/gamma/gain + temp/tint/sat/contrast/curves (the 90% of a primary) first.

### 4.2 Editor ‚Äî Pro NLE Timeline

**Target:** a real multi-track timeline (named A/B-roll/overlay/text/audio tracks, clips with source-vs-timeline time, ripple/roll/slip/slide, J/L cuts, markers) as a **non-destructive layer on `ContentItem`**, exactly the way `Edl` lives today. A deterministic `compileTimeline` projects it back onto the existing prop-driven `Post` composition ‚Äî zero render rewrite for v1.

**Current state:** NO timeline ‚Äî flat `Storyboard.scenes: Scene[]` (schemas:545) played strictly sequentially. ONE music + ONE voice track + per-cue sfx (Post.tsx:1073-1080); "tracks" are only `Mix.tracks` of 3 fixed ids carrying global vol/mute/speed/fade (schemas:763), not clips. The closest to clip timing is the derived read-only `sceneStarts()`/`TimelineScene` (editor-tools.ts:132). Scenes have `hidden`/`locked` (schemas:157) but no in/out points ‚Äî a scene IS its full duration. `Edl.decisions` is one `EdlDecision` per scene (schemas:~908).

**Data model:** `Timeline` / `Track{kind,role,index,clips}` / `Clip{sceneRef,timelineInSec,durationSec,sourceInSec,sourceDurationSec,speed,fade,enabled,locked}` / `Marker` (new) ‚Üí `ContentItem.timeline?` (optional ‚Üí all legacy runs parse); `EdlDecision += clipId?`.

**Render changes:**
- `creative/compile.ts` (new) `compileTimeline(id)`: walk A/B video tracks in timeline order ‚Üí flat ordered `Scene[]` (clone the storyboard scene at `clip.sceneRef`, set `durationSec` from clip, honor speed, order by `timelineInSec`); gaps ‚Üí synthesized hold scene; B/overlay/text clips ‚Üí base scene's `overlays[]`/`broll`; audio clips ‚Üí `Mix` + positioned cues. Write back to `item.storyboard` (through `Storyboard.parse`) + `item.mix`. **Pure, deterministic, locked-safe ‚Äî copies `applyEdlToStoryboard`'s contract exactly, including its `pacingScale` rescale (edl.ts:291) so ripple never violates the [12,75]s `superRefine` (schemas:559).**
- **J/L cuts need NO `TransitionSeries` change** ‚Äî audio already renders as independent `<Audio from=‚Ä¶>` (the sfx pattern at Post.tsx:1075 generalizes), so a voice clip starting before/after its picture is a positioned `<Audio>`. *This is the key realization that makes the signature DaVinci move reachable cheaply.*
- v2: `TimelineComposition.tsx` (`id='Timeline'`, plain `<Sequence from=‚Ä¶>` + manual crossfades) ‚Äî same component family as the M0 fix; `render.ts` (render.ts:140) selects `'Timeline'` when `timeline.compiledAt` is set, else `'Post'`.

**EDL bridge:** the timeline is the editor's CANVAS, the Edl its INTENT ‚Äî they compose. Each `EdlDecision` ‚Üí one A-track clip (`durationSec` = decision pacing result, `transition` = `transitionIn`, `sceneRef` = `sceneId`). `mixIntent` "voice leads picture 0.4s" ‚Üí a J-cut clip. A trim that violates the readability floor is flagged by `editSignals`'s `recommendMinSec` (signals.ts:154) the same way pacing is today.

**AI pass:** a TIMELINE PASS after assembly/pacing operating on real clips: ripple-trim dead air after VO ends (`signals.deadAir`), add a 0.3s L-cut, slip b-roll so its action peak lands on a `beatFrame`, razor at a detected hard cut. Each = a structured `TimelineOp` (JSON-at-boundary like `EdlDecision`), applied through the trim tools (ripple math + clamps enforced once), compiled, optionally re-rendered + `editor_compare_renders` verified. OBSERVE ‚Üí DECIDE ‚Üí APPLY ‚Üí REVIEW unchanged; the *unit of action* upgrades from scene-param to clip-on-track.

**New tools:** `timeline_build`, `timeline_get`, `timeline_trim` (ripple/roll/slip/slide), `timeline_razor`, `timeline_insert_overwrite`, `timeline_jl_cut`, `timeline_marker`, `timeline_compile` ‚Äî all spread into registry.ts.

**Risks:** three projections (edl‚Üístoryboard, timeline‚Üístoryboard, manual scene edits) can fight ‚Üí the canonical order + "timeline owns timing once compiledAt" precedence rule (¬ß2.1) is the fix; `slip` only means anything for source-backed clips (broll/voice), gate it off synthesized "scene" clips; per-clip speed needs per-clip `<Audio playbackRate>` (v2); the `TransitionSeries` bug blocks true overlapping PICTURE (v1 projects B/overlay onto the base scene; real overlap waits on `id='Timeline'`).

### 4.3 Mixer ‚Äî Fairlight-level audio desk

**Target:** turn today's two-volume mix into a real desk ‚Äî keyframed gain/pan automation per track and per clip, a per-track EQ/comp/de-ess/de-noise/gate chain, a stereo bus with limiter + the existing -14 LUFS master, and word-aware sidechain ducking ‚Äî judged by REAL meters.

**Current state:** SHORTS mix live in Post.tsx ‚Äî `music = trackVolume √ó musicArc(f) √ó duckEnvelope(f) √ó fadeVolume`; `buildDuckEnvelope` (Post.tsx:177) ramps over `WordCue` spans; `pan`/`splits` schema-only, unused. LONG-FORM = `render.ts addMusicBed` single ffmpeg pass (volume ‚Üí sidechaincompress ‚Üí amix ‚Üí loudnorm -14). Pre-bake in media.ts: `polishVoice` (hardcoded EQ string), `duckMusic`, `ensureMusic`, `buildSfxCues`. `masterAudio` = loudnorm I=-14:TP=-1.5:LRA=11. Evidence: `volumedetect`/`silencedetect`/`showwavespic` ‚Äî **no LUFS/TP/LRA/per-region**. The `audio` pass (passes.ts:117) emits only free-text `mixIntent`; `parseMixIntent` (edl.ts:537) maps to coarse `duck`/`musicVol`/`voiceVol`.

**Data model:** `GainPoint`/`AutoCurve` (keyframed gain/pan, `t‚àà0..1`, evaluated like `resolveKeyframes`); `AudioBand` (parametric EQ); `Comp`/`DeEss`/`Gate`/`Denoise`; `AudioTrack` += `automation`/`eq`/`comp`/`gate`/`deEss`/`denoise`/`bus`/`limiterCeilingDb`; `AudioClip` (new ‚Äî per-region overrides); `Mix` += `clips`/`loudnessTarget`/`duck.mode('sidechain'|'keyed')`/`busses`; `AudioMeter` (evidence, not persisted).

**Render changes:**
- Post.tsx: generalize `fadeVolume` (Post.tsx:147) ‚Üí `evalAutomation(frame, span, curves, base)` (same engine as `resolveKeyframes`/`buildDuckEnvelope`); music/voice/sfx `<Audio volume>` multiply in `automation('gain')`; per-clip automation via extra `<Sequence>`.
- media.ts: new `buildAudioFiltergraph(track|clip)` ‚Äî deterministic ffmpeg `-af` from EQ (`equalizer`/shelf), comp (`acompressor`), de-ess (`deesser`), denoise (`afftdn`), gate (`agate`), pan, limiter (`alimiter`). `polishVoice` BUILDS from the voice track's chain instead of the hardcoded string (current values = defaults). `duckMusic` gains a `keyed` mode baking the SAME word-span envelope as `buildDuckEnvelope` ‚Üí **long-form gets word-accurate ducking**.
- **Shared duck module:** extract `mergeSpans` + `buildDuckEnvelope` from Post.tsx into a lib both Post.tsx (JS per-frame) and media.ts (ffmpeg) import ‚Äî the only thing keeping shorts + long-form ducking identical.
- `render.ts`: `addMusicBed`/`masterAudio` read `mix.loudnessTarget` instead of hardcoded loudnorm.

**EDL bridge:** enrich `parseMixIntent` to emit chains: *"muddy/boxy"*‚ÜíEQ peak -2.5dB@250 + presence +3@3.5k; *"essy"*‚Üíde-ess 4‚Äì6dB@6.5k; *"hiss"*‚Üídenoise 0.3‚Äì0.5; *"let silence breathe"*‚Üíper-clip `AutoCurve` gain dip scoped to that scene's frame span (compute `fromF/toF` from durations the bridge already knows); *"punchy/glued"*‚Üímaster bus comp + limiter. Loudness target platform-derived from `EditBrief.platform`. All clamped ‚Äî the AI can never emit an unsafe EQ/comp.

**AI pass (grounded in METERS):** PERCEIVE (`editor_analyze_av` extended: integrated/short-term LUFS via `ebur128`, true-peak, LRA, per-region VO/bed/silence RMS) ‚Üí DIAGNOSE vs targets (VO-in-gap vs under-bed delta ‚â• ~9 LU, TP ‚â§ -1 dBTP, integrated within 0.5 LU of target, music LRA showing real dynamics not a flat wall) ‚Üí DECIDE (`mixIntent`) ‚Üí APPLY ‚Üí VERIFY (`compareDiagnostics` at editor-tools.ts:325, extended with LUFS/TP deltas). **Conservative: a chain that worsens integrated LUFS or pushes TP hot is skipped, never thrown** (passes contract). Add an explicit "dynamics preserved" check (LRA not collapsed) beyond loudness.

**New tools:** `mix_get`/`mix_set_track`, `mix_automation`, `mix_duck`, `mix_meter` (the `editor_analyze_av` LUFS extension), `mix_apply`, `mix_normalize_loudness`.

**Risks:** browser can't do EQ/comp ‚Üí all spectral/dynamics pre-baked by ffmpeg before the file reaches `<Audio>`; **only gain/pan automation is JS-expressible in-render** ‚Äî the resolver must own which stage applies what or double-application; two render paths drift unless every mix change goes through both builders; per-region LUFS is slow on long-form (use `astats` RMS as cheap proxy); de-ess/de-noise on clean TTS may harm ‚Äî reach for restoration only when meters show sibilance/hiss; pan is mono-summed on phone speakers ‚Äî likely long-form/headphone-only, keep shorts mono-safe. **This subsystem is independent of the TransitionSeries bug** and can run in parallel after M0.

### 4.4 Compositor ‚Äî Fusion-level node graph

**Target:** replace flat `scene.effects` booleans + global `ColorGrade` with a composable `EffectGraph` (DAG: source ‚Üí mask ‚Üí key ‚Üí glow/blur/sharpen ‚Üí blend ‚Üí displace ‚Üí tracker-attach) with per-param keyframe tracks, rendered as layered `AbsoluteFill` + SVG `<filter>` + CSS blend modes ‚Äî with an ffmpeg pre-bake escape hatch for true keys/tracking.

**Current state:** compositing is FLAT across three disconnected layers: (1) `scene.effects` boolean passthrough (grain/vignette/contrast/scanlines/blur/invert) ‚Äî NOT on the validated schema, read by `sceneFilter` (Post.tsx:452) + `SceneEffectOverlay` (Post.tsx:431), written by `editor_set_effect` via raw JSON; (2) global `<ColorGrade>` + `LightLeak` + `ChromaDefs` `#chroma` filter (grade.tsx, applied to b-roll at Post.tsx:691); (3) free-form `scene.overlays[]` (Post.tsx:639) ‚Äî static, no keyframes, no masking/blending/tracking. Keyframes exist but ONLY for scene-text transform (`resolveKeyframes`, lib/keyframes.ts, consumed at Post.tsx:735). The perception substrate already exists: `editor_video_evidence` (editor-tools.ts:~1996, dense frames + OCR + motion + pixel metrics).

**Data model:** `EffectNode{type(enum of ~22),params,blend,mask,inputs,keyframes}` / `EffectGraph{nodes(max 24),output,scope}` (cycles rejected via `refine()`) / `TrackData{kind,samples}` (new) ‚Üí `sceneBase.style.comp?` + `Storyboard.comp?` (scope:'post'); `EdlDecision += visualIntent`.

**Render changes:**
- `lib/comp/CompositeGraph.tsx` (new): topo-sort + render the DAG as layered `AbsoluteFills`. Each node ‚Üí a primitive: blur/sharpen/glow/bloom/chroma_ab/displace ‚Üí per-node SVG `<filter>` (`feGaussianBlur`, `feConvolveMatrix`, `feColorMatrix+feComposite` glow, `feDisplacementMap+feTurbulence`); blend ‚Üí `mixBlendMode`; mask_shape ‚Üí `<clipPath>`/`<mask>`; mask_luma ‚Üí luminance‚Üíalpha; key_chroma/key_luma ‚Üí `feColorMatrix` knockout (flag despill limits); **grade ‚Üí a real per-scene grade node (lift/gamma/gain/sat/temp) ‚Äî the same field the Color subsystem adds, unified here**; light_leak/grain/vignette ‚Üí reuse grade.tsx primitives as nodes.
- Generalize `resolveKeyframes` ‚Üí `resolveParamTracks(tracks, frame, durF)` keyed by arbitrary param name (blurPx, glowAmount, leakX‚Ä¶), reusing existing easing/interp/hold.
- `SceneInner` (Post.tsx:716): if `style.comp` exists, wrap content in `<CompositeGraph>`, else fall back to `sceneFilter`+`SceneEffectOverlay`. Post level (Post.tsx:1063): if `storyboard.comp` (scope post), replace the `ColorGrade`/treatment stack with a post-scope graph.

**EDL bridge:** `buildCompFromIntents(scene, decision)` reads `colorIntent` + `visualIntent` + `emphasis` + `fn` ‚Üí deterministically assembles a small `EffectGraph`: *"glow/dreamy"*‚Üíglow node (0‚Äì0.6); *"isolate subject"*‚Üíkey_luma/chroma + glow (flag: CSS key approximate); *"track X to Y"*‚Üí`track_attach` node + `TrackData` request; *"teal-orange"*‚Üígrade node. Every param clamped; locked scene untouched; bad intent skipped with a note.

**AI pass (9th, after `visual`):** PERCEIVE (`editor_video_evidence` ‚Äî already works) ‚Üí judge per scene (subject lost against busy b-roll ‚Üí key+glow; flat peak ‚Üí light leak + bloom; logo should ride the phone ‚Üí track+attach) ‚Üí emit `visualIntent` OR wire nodes via `comp_*` tools ‚Üí re-render + `creative_perceive` verify against `creative_taste`. **Grounded, never blind:** node params proposed only where evidence shows a deficiency (low subject contrast from pixel metrics, flat motion deltas). DNA-learned looks (`genomeContextSafe`) bias defaults per channel.

**New tools:** `comp_graph_set`, `comp_node_add`, `comp_node_wire`, `comp_node_keyframe`, `comp_track` (offline ffmpeg+opencv tracker ‚Üí `TrackData`, detached-spawn `{status:'started',pid,logPath}`), `comp_prebake` (ffmpeg despill/optical-flow/lens-distortion escape hatch, detached-spawn), `comp_preview` (single composited frame).

**Risks:** TransitionSeries bug multiplies per-scene filter graphs ~16√ó ‚Üí **ship post-scope graphs first** (outside TransitionSeries), gate heavy per-scene graphs behind M0; SVG filter perf at 1080√ó1920/frame is heavy ‚Üí node budget (max 24, ~6 filter-heavy), pre-bake expensive ones; CSS/SVG keying is approximate (no real despill) ‚Üí route true keys to `comp_prebake`; **the graph MUST be schema-real** (unlike `scene.effects` passthrough) or `Storyboard.parse` strips it; tracking must be precomputed offline (`TrackData`), never in React render, or renders are non-deterministic; over-authored graphs ‚Üí muddy look, mitigate with taste-scored verify + DNA restraint + opacity subtlety clamp.

---

**Front-loading logic:** M0 unblocks the most (3 of 4 crafts' evidence). M1 defines the single write surface so the four crafts can't fragment. M2 is the smallest unlock for the highest-value craft (color). **Audio (M6‚ÄìM9) runs fully in parallel** ‚Äî it doesn't touch TransitionSeries. Compositing's *post-scope* delivery (M13) is sequenced before its heavy per-scene work (M14) precisely because post-scope sits outside the buggy render path.

## 6. Risks & open decisions for the human

**Decisions to make before building (these change the architecture):**

1. **Single source of truth once a timeline exists.** *Recommendation: timeline wins after first `timeline_build`; the Edl becomes intent-only for non-timing axes.* Sign-off needed ‚Äî this is the precedence rule that prevents the three-projection desync, the program's biggest structural risk.
2. **Global grade authorship vs the gate.** *Recommendation: agent writes per-scene grades freely + a *suggested* `storyboard.grade`; the global trim is human-approvable* ‚Äî consistent with "gates are sacred." Confirm.
3. **HSL qualifier / real secondaries in color v1?** *Recommendation: defer.* Ship lift/gamma/gain + temp/tint/sat/contrast/curves (90% of a DaVinci primary) before risking over-promising SVG secondaries.
4. **Pre-look vs post-look scopes.** Likely need both: "primary" scopes pre-look (grade only) to grade against, "delivery" scopes post-look (grade + overlay + grain) for QA. Confirm the loop reads the pre-look frame for convergence.
5. **Linear-light vs sRGB grading.** *Recommendation: accept sRGB `feComponentTransfer` and tune mappers to measured scope targets* (the loop optimizes toward measured numbers, not a reference math model). Revisit only if visual-diff against a reference grade is unacceptable.
6. **Per-clip audio granularity.** Today there's ONE voice file (`synthVoiceSceneSynced` assembles all lines). True per-line clip control means *not* pre-mixing VO ‚Äî a bigger pipeline change. *Recommendation: ship `mix.clips[]` frame-region overrides first; defer multi-file-per-track.*
7. **Pan on 9:16.** Phone speakers mono-sum. *Recommendation: pan is long-form/headphone-only; keep shorts mono-safe.*
8. **Compositing grade node vs the Color subsystem's `scene.style.grade`.** These overlap deliberately ‚Äî *recommendation: ONE grade implementation* (`scene.style.grade` from ¬ß4.1), exposed as a `grade` node type in CompositeGraph, not two grade systems.
9. **Long-form timeline scope.** ChapterBoard renders per-chapter then concats (render.ts:292). *Recommendation: one timeline per chapter* (matches the existing render-then-concat path).
10. **Tracking depth.** *Recommendation: start with cheap template-match/feature tracking* (logos onto phones, labels onto objects); escalate to optical-flow only if evidence demands.

**Cross-cutting risks to watch during execution:**

- **Frame-offset coupling (M0).** `totalFrames` + duck envelope + sfx `atF` + beat frames must stay byte-identical through the manual-crossfade rewrite or captions/voice desync. Snapshot-test `totalFrames` per transition.
- **Two render paths drift (audio).** Shorts live-mix vs long-form ffmpeg post-pass stay identical *only* through the shared duck-span + filtergraph builders. Every mix change must traverse both.
- **Schema strip (compositing).** `scene.effects` survives today only because `editor_set_effect` uses raw JSON. The new `comp`/`grade` fields MUST be on the validated schema (M1) or `Storyboard.parse` in round-trip/validation paths silently drops them. *Verified: `sceneBase.style` is NOT `.strict()`, so additive optional fields are safe ‚Äî but they must be declared, not passthrough.*
- **Over-processing.** An agent that grades + keys + glows + leaks every scene produces a muddy, over-glued result. The taste-scored verify loop + DNA restraint defaults + opacity/LRA "subtlety" clamps are the only guardrails ‚Äî keep them load-bearing.
---

## 7. Pillar 5 ‚Äî Ingest & Understand (arbitrary user video) *(added 2026-06-14)*

**Requirement (user):** the AI must *take in any video, understand it deeply, subtitle it, edit it, and re-montage it on request* ‚Äî the Odysser model (content-aware analysis ‚Üí chat-driven augmentation, not timeline-first). Socheli today GENERATES video; this pillar lets it INGEST one.

**Reference ‚Äî Odysser (odysser.com):** content-aware analysis (script/lighting/framing) ‚Üí storyboard ‚Üí element-by-element animation ‚Üí integrate; **chat-based editing** ("tweak by chat, not After Effects"); approval system (guided ‚Üî autonomous agent); brand/style consistency; learns from interactions. Its chat-first + approval + workflow-step UI is the reference for our Edit page (M17).

**Reuse (already exists / in flight):** Whisper word-level transcription (`media.ts`), ffmpeg scene-change detect (`editor_analyze_av`), `creative/perception.ts` `analyzeClip` (motion/quality/best-moment), the NLE `Timeline`/`compileTimeline` (M10‚ÄìM16), color/audio/compositing passes, Soli chat + editor role + `creative_*` tools.

**New work (the ingest front-end):**
- **N1 ‚Äî Import & probe:** accept a user video (path/upload), ffprobe (codec/res/fps/duration/audio), normalize, register as a new `ContentItem` of `kind:"ingested"` with `sourceVideo`. Schema: `ContentItem.source` (path, probe), reuse `Timeline`.
- **N2 ‚Äî Deep understanding:** transcribe (Whisper ‚Üí word/segment timestamps + speaker turns); shot/scene segmentation (scene-change + silence + speaker boundaries); per-shot multimodal analysis (subject, faces, framing, motion, quality, on-screen text/OCR, dead air, highlight/peak moments, filler/‚Äúum‚Äù detection). Persist as `item.understanding` (a structured index the agent reads).
- **N3 ‚Äî Seed timeline from footage:** build `item.timeline` from real clips (one per shot, in/out at source time), an audio track from the source, a caption track from the transcript. Now the existing trim/ripple/J-L/compile machinery applies to REAL footage.
- **N4 ‚Äî Auto-subtitle:** transcript ‚Üí styled caption track (reuse the karaoke/caption render + caption presets); editable.
- **N5 ‚Äî Agent edit/montage on real video:** extend the passes + Soli to operate on the ingested timeline ‚Äî "cut the dead air", "tighten to 60s", "re-montage as a fast-cut highlight reel", "subtitle it", "add b-roll on the gaps", "grade it warm", "mix the VO up". Each is timeline ops + color/audio/comp passes the program already builds, grounded in `item.understanding` evidence.
- **N6 ‚Äî Render the edit:** a render path that cuts the SOURCE video per the compiled timeline (ffmpeg concat/trim of real footage + Remotion overlay layer for captions/b-roll/graphics/grade) ‚Äî hybrid: ffmpeg for the footage spine, Remotion for the augmentation layer.

**Sequencing:** N1‚ÄìN4 (ingest + understand + subtitle) can start after M1 (timeline schema, done) and run parallel to color/audio. N5 leans on the NLE trims (M11) + the craft passes. N6 (hybrid footage render) is the deepest new piece (ffmpeg-cut source + Remotion overlay). To be planned in detail by a dedicated architect workflow.


## 7.1 Pillar 5 ‚Äî Ingest & Understand: the detailed build plan *(synthesized 2026-06-14)*

> This expands the ¬ß7 stub into a buildable, milestone-by-milestone plan. It is grounded in the real code (every file/line below was read), opinionated where the five source designs disagreed, and sequenced so each milestone has a demoable outcome and ships behind the existing gates.

### 7.1.1 Vision

Socheli generates faceless video today. Pillar 5 makes it **ingest any user video, understand it deeply, subtitle it, and edit/re-montage it from chat** ‚Äî the Odysser experience (content-aware analysis ‚Üí chat-driven augmentation ‚Üí guided/autonomous approval ‚Üí learns), realized on our existing **Remotion + ffmpeg** stack rather than a new editor.

The architectural bet that makes this ~70% reuse: **register an ingested video as a normal `ContentItem`** (`kind:"ingested"`, with `videoPath` = the normalized source). The moment it's a ContentItem, every evidence tool (`editor_analyze_av`, `videoDiagnostics`, `analyzeClip`, `denseFrameScan`, `runVisionOcr`), every craft pass (color M2‚ÄìM5, audio M6‚ÄìM9), the NLE timeline (`Clip`/`Track`/`Timeline` already on `ContentItem.timeline`), the caption renderer (Post.tsx `Karaoke`/`SubtitleLayer`), and the agent loop (`passes.ts` ‚Üí `edl.ts` bridge ‚Üí `review.ts` ‚Üí `taste.ts`/`perf.ts`) operate on real footage **unchanged**. The genuinely new code is a thin spine: ingest front-door (N1), an understanding index the agent reads (N2), a timeline+caption seed from footage (N3/N4), a footage-mode branch + montage + intent-router for the agent (N5), and a hybrid footage render (N6).

The chat unit of action shifts from "a generated Scene" to "a clip-on-track / a footage region" ‚Äî but it flows through the same clamped, locked-safe, evidence-grounded machinery, so the gates and the "never taste alone" discipline carry over intact.

### 7.1.2 Data model ‚Äî one coherent set of additions

All additions are **`.optional()` / additive** so every legacy run still `ContentItem.parse()`s. Verified attach points: `Clip`/`Track`/`Timeline` at `schemas/index.ts:1276‚Äì1317`, `ContentItem` at `:1332` (already carries `timeline`, `mix`, `videoPath`, `thumbPath`, `clipAnalysis`, `kind`), `Lifecycle` at `:1320`, `Mix.subtitles` at `:990`, `ClipAnalysis` at `:1206`.

**(a) Source ‚Äî `ContentItem.source` (N1 owns).** One new block:

```ts
// @os/schemas ‚Äî new
export const SourceProbe = z.object({
  container: z.string().optional(),
  durationSec: z.number(),
  video: z.object({
    codec: z.string(), width: z.number().int(), height: z.number().int(),
    fps: z.number(),                       // from avg_frame_rate
    rotation: z.number().int().default(0), // side_data_list ‚à™ tags.rotate ‚à™ display-matrix
    pixFmt: z.string().optional(), sar: z.string().optional(), bitrate: z.number().optional(),
  }).optional(),
  audioStreams: z.array(z.object({
    codec: z.string(), channels: z.number().int(),
    sampleRate: z.number().int(), language: z.string().optional(),
  })).default([]),
  hasAudio: z.boolean().default(false),
});
export const SourceVideo = z.object({
  originalPath: z.string(), originalName: z.string().optional(),
  path: z.string(),                        // render-friendly file (== originalPath if no transcode)
  normalized: z.boolean().default(false), normalizeReason: z.string().optional(),
  bytes: z.number().int().optional(), sha256: z.string().optional(),
  probe: SourceProbe, importedAt: z.string(), importedBy: z.string().optional(),
});
// ContentItem += source: SourceVideo.optional()
// kind enum += "ingested"   (schemas:1341)
// Lifecycle  += "ingested"  (schemas:1320 ‚Äî resting state after import)
```

**(b) Understanding ‚Äî `ContentItem.understanding` (N2 owns; N3/N5 read).** The structured index the agent reads, mirroring how `editSignals` grounds the generated passes today. **Store SECONDS throughout** (source-accurate); convert to frames only at caption build (N4):

```ts
// @os/schemas ‚Äî new
const Span = z.object({ startSec: z.number(), endSec: z.number(), reason: z.string().optional() });
const TWord = z.object({ word: z.string(), startSec: z.number(), endSec: z.number(), conf: z.number().optional() });
const TSegment = z.object({ index: z.number().int(), startSec: z.number(), endSec: z.number(),
  text: z.string(), speaker: z.string().optional(), avgLogprob: z.number().optional(), noSpeechProb: z.number().optional() });
const Transcript = z.object({ text: z.string(), words: z.array(TWord).default([]), segments: z.array(TSegment).default([]) });
const Speaker = z.object({ id: z.string(), label: z.string().optional(), turns: z.array(Span).default([]), totalSec: z.number() });
const Shot = z.object({ id: z.string(), index: z.number().int(),
  inSec: z.number(), outSec: z.number(), durationSec: z.number(),
  source: z.enum(["cut","silence","speaker","fallback"]),  // which boundary opened it
  keyframeSec: z.number(), speaker: z.string().optional() });
const ShotAnalysis = ClipAnalysis.extend({           // reuse motion/shaky/quality/brightness/bestMomentSec
  onScreenText: z.string().optional(), ocrConf: z.number().optional(),
  framing: z.enum(["wide","mid","tight"]).optional(), // coarse, from edge/contrast metrics
  motionDelta: z.number().optional(), transcriptText: z.string().optional(),
  energyRms: z.number().optional(), faces: z.number().int().optional() /* absent v1; no detector in-repo */ });
const Highlight = z.object({ startSec: z.number(), endSec: z.number(), score: z.number(), why: z.array(z.string()).default([]) });
const FillerHit = z.object({ atSec: z.number(), word: z.string(), kind: z.enum(["filler","long_pause"]) });
const RedundantPair = z.object({ aSeg: z.number().int(), bSeg: z.number().int(), similarity: z.number() });
export const Understanding = z.object({
  builtAt: z.string(), durationSec: z.number(), fps: z.number().optional(),
  transcript: Transcript, speakers: z.array(Speaker).default([]),
  shots: z.array(Shot).default([]), perShot: z.record(z.string(), ShotAnalysis).default({}),
  highlights: z.array(Highlight).default([]), deadAir: z.array(Span).default([]),
  filler: z.array(FillerHit).default([]), redundancy: z.array(RedundantPair).default([]),
  notes: z.array(z.string()).default([]),  // e.g. "heuristic speaker turns", "no audio"
});
// ContentItem += understanding: Understanding.optional()
```

**(c) Timeline ‚Äî reuse `ContentItem.timeline` (N3 writes), small additive extensions.** No new top-level field. Extend `Clip`, `Mix.subtitles`, `Timeline`:

```ts
// Clip += (caption payload, so a kind:"text" clip carries the words it shows)
captionText: z.string().optional(),
words: z.array(z.object({ word: z.string(), fromSec: z.number(), toSec: z.number() })).optional(),
// Mix.subtitles += source: z.enum(["voice","track"]).optional()  // "track" ‚Üí render reads the caption track
// Timeline += seededFrom: z.enum(["storyboard","footage"]).optional()  // tells compile/render which spine
```

**(d) Agent layer ‚Äî `EditPlan`/`EditOp`/`MontageSpec` (N5 owns), `EditingTaste` extension.** The Odysser "proposed edits" surface + the new edit gate operate on `EditPlan`; persisted as flat JSON under `data/` like every other run artifact:

```ts
const EditOp = z.discriminatedUnion("kind", [ /* ripple_trim | razor | jl_cut | slip | insert_broll |
  subtitle | grade | mix | select_highlight | reorder ‚Äî each maps 1:1 to an existing trim tool / pass / bridge mapper */ ]);
const EditPlan = z.object({ id: z.string(), runId: z.string(), request: z.string(),
  mode: z.enum(["guided","autonomous"]), ops: z.array(EditOp), rationale: z.string(),
  evidenceRefs: z.array(z.string()),  // shot ids / silence regions / scope refs it cites
  estDurationSec: z.number().optional(),
  status: z.enum(["proposed","approved","applied","rejected"]), createdAt: z.string() });
const MontageSpec = z.object({ runId: z.string(), targetSec: z.number().optional(),
  style: z.enum(["highlight_reel","teaser","tighten","supercut"]),
  order: z.enum(["narrative","energy","chronological"]),
  maxClips: z.number().int().optional(), perClipMinSec: z.number().optional(),
  mustKeepShotIds: z.array(z.string()).optional() });
// EditingTaste += learned footage prefs (reel-length band, cut cadence, trim aggressiveness) alongside colorTargets
```

> **Opinionated calls baked into the model.** (1) Captions reuse the existing **`kind:"text"`** track with a `track.name="captions"` convention ‚Äî **no new track-enum member** (avoids a schema enum churn). (2) For ingested runs the **timeline is authoritative** (`timeline.compiledAt` set, `seededFrom:"footage"`); the `Edl` is **not used** as a second projection ‚Äî `EditPlan/EditOp` is the intent layer, the timeline is realization. This kills the "three projections fighting" desync the roadmap ¬ß2.1 warns about. (3) `Understanding` is a **one-shot ingest artifact**; the mutable layer is `timeline`. (4) `faces` stays optional-absent (grep confirms **no face detector in-repo**) ‚Äî framing is approximated from edge/contrast pixel metrics only; don't overclaim face analysis.

### 7.1.3 End-to-end pipeline ‚Äî REUSE vs NEW per stage

```
content import ‚Üí ingest_video (N1)
   ‚Üí editor_understand (N2)
      ‚Üí ingest_seed_timeline + auto_subtitle (N3/N4)
         ‚Üí creative_edit_route ‚Üí [edit gate] ‚Üí creative_apply_plan / creative_montage (N5)
            ‚Üí timeline_compile (M10) ‚Üí render_hybrid (N6)
               ‚Üí hybrid_qa ‚Üí [publish gate]
```

| Stage | REUSE (verified) | NEW |
|---|---|---|
| **N1 Import & probe** | `editor-tools.ts:160 ffprobe`, `:200 durationFromProbe`; `render.ts:206 coverBg` (thumb), `:297 concatVideos`/`:~306 libx264` settings (normalize recipe); `store.ts` `newId`/`saveItem`/`loadItem`/`logLine`/`warn` + `RENDERS_DIR` (large-file volume); `helpers.ts` `tool`/`ok`/`fail`/`spawnEngine`; dashboard `app/api/{media,transcribe,assets}` upload/serve precedent | `ingest.ts` (`resolveSource`/`probeSource`/`needsNormalize`/`normalizeVideo`/`thumbnailSource`/`importVideo`); `SourceVideo`/`SourceProbe` schema + `kind:"ingested"` + `ingested` Lifecycle; `INGEST_DIR = join(RENDERS_DIR,"..","ingest")`; `ingest-tools.ts` (`ingest_video`/`ingest_probe`/`ingest_status`); `content import` CLI case; `app/api/ingest` route |
| **N2 Understand** | `editor-tools.ts:1135 transcribeVideoAudio` (+ `:1129 extractAudioForTranscript`) ‚Äî the **single** Whisper path to reuse; `scripts/whisper-words.py`; `:299 videoDiagnostics` (scene/silence/freeze/black/ebur128/regionRms); `perception.ts:177 analyzeClip`; `:204 denseFrameScan`, `:1099 runVisionOcr`, `:936 analyzeFramePixels`, `:1149 wordsInRange`, `:1089 textSimilarity`; `signals.ts:251 editSignals` dead-air model + `:287 signalsSummary` (pattern for `understandingSummary`) | `whisper-words.py` emits **segments[]** (currently flattens to words, drops boundaries ‚Äî **load-bearing edit**); `Understanding` + sub-schemas; **heuristic speaker-turn segmenter** (pause-gap + RMS level-shift, NOT diarization); **shot-boundary fusion** (sceneChanges ‚à™ silence ‚à™ speaker-turns ‚Üí min-length-absorbed shots, single-take fallback); per-shot assembler; editorial scorers (filler regex+gaps, highlight composite, redundancy, deadAir); `understandingSummary()`; `editor_understand` (detached) + `editor_understanding_get` |
| **N3 Seed timeline** | `Clip`/`Track`/`Marker`/`Timeline` (source-time `inSec/outSec` + timeline-time `startSec/durationSec/speed` already fit footage); `creative/timeline.ts:80 timelineView` (the read shape N3 **writes**); `ClipAnalysis` (perception payload per real clip) | `creative/seed-from-footage.ts` `buildTimelineFromFootage(id)` (V1 video clips at source time, A1 source audio, CAP1 caption clips; copies per-shot perception to `clipAnalysis`; dead-air/filler **Markers**; idempotent re-seed keyed by **stable shot id, not array index**); `ingest_seed_timeline` tool |
| **N4 Subtitle** | `Post.tsx:199 buildGroups` (word‚Üíline idiom), `Karaoke`(WordCue)/`SubtitleLayer`(SubtitleCue), `Mix.subtitles` presets; `timeline.ts toFrames` (sec‚Üíframe **round**) | `creative/auto-subtitle.ts` (transcript‚Üígrouped text Clips on CAP1 + sets `Mix.subtitles.source="track"`); caption-Clip(sec)‚ÜíWordCue[](frames) compile mapping; `auto_subtitle`/`caption_edit`/`caption_restyle`/`caption_translate` tools |
| **N5 Edit/montage + chat** | `passes.ts runPass`/`mergePass`/`PASS_FIELDS`; `edl.ts applyEdlToStoryboard`/`parseMixIntent`/`colorIntentToGrade`/`gradeScene`/`gradeGlobal`; `signals.ts`; `review.ts reviewCut`; `perf.ts learnTasteFromReview`; `loop.ts`; `brain.ts think`; Soli `lib/agent/tools.ts runTool` + `ui-spec.ts` (storyboard/diff/scorecard/gate/steps blocks); `harness/roles.ts editor` | **footage-mode branch** in `passes.ts` (assembly/pacing/visual/typography emit `EditOp`s over real clips, not an Edl over scenes); `creative/edit-router.ts routeEditRequest`; `creative/apply-plan.ts applyEditPlan`; `creative/montage.ts` (`selectHighlights`+`orderMontage`); `EditPlan`/`EditOp`/`MontageSpec` schemas; `creative_edit_route`/`creative_apply_plan`/`creative_montage`/`creative_subtitle`/`creative_edit_plan_{get,approve,reject}`; footage-aware editor role + Soli prompt |
| **N6 Hybrid render** | `render.ts:297 concatVideos`, `:315 addMusicBed` (duck+EQ+loudnorm), `:27 masterAudio`, `:127 renderPost` (bundle/sidecar/retry); `Post.tsx:699 BrollBackground` **OffthreadVideo base** (the footage-base mechanism); `grade.tsx GradePipeline`/`gradeToFilterId` (grade on footage for free); `media.ts:225 buildAudioFiltergraph`, `:41/:61 build/mergeDuckSpans`; longform render-then-concat precedent | `creative/compile.ts compileTimeline` (M10 dep ‚Äî minimal footage-only compiler first); `render.ts renderHybrid`+`cutClip`+`buildFootageAudio`; `HybridPost.tsx` composition (OffthreadVideo spine + GradePipeline + reused overlay layers; falls back to `Post` byte-identical when `spineSrc` absent); `Root.tsx` registration + `calculateMetadata`; `HybridPostProps`; render dispatch on `kind==="ingested" || timeline.compiledAt`; `render_hybrid`/`timeline_compile`/`render_spine_preview`/`hybrid_qa` tools |

### 7.1.4 Hybrid render architecture ‚Äî DECISION

**Decision: ffmpeg-first footage spine ‚Üí a single Remotion `HybridPost` composition that composites EVERYTHING over the cut spine via an `OffthreadVideo` base layer ‚Üí grade applied on the footage layer **inside Remotion** ‚Üí audio built entirely by ffmpeg and muxed last.** One video encode through Remotion, one audio pass through ffmpeg, one stream-copy mux.

Seven steps, one `renderHybrid(id)` in `render.ts`:
1. **Compile ‚Üí spine plan.** `compileTimeline(id)` walks the video track in `startSec` order ‚Üí ordered `clipPlan` of `{src,inSec,outSec,speed,durationSec}`; for ingested clips `src = item.source.path`. WxH/fps from `source.probe`.
2. **ffmpeg cuts each clip** (`-ss inSec -i src -t dur -vf "scale=W:H:force_original_aspect_ratio=decrease,pad,setsar=1[,setpts=PTS/speed]" -an -r FPS -c:v libx264 -crf 18`). Audio stripped; **re-encode each part** so it starts at frame 0 (frame-accurate seams), normalize to one timebase/codec/WxH/fps/`yuv420p`/`bt709`.
3. **ffmpeg concats parts ‚Üí silent spine** ‚Äî reuse `concatVideos(id+'_spine', parts)` **verbatim**.
4. **Build `HybridPost` props** ‚Äî **symlink** the spine into `remotion/public/` (reuse the broll symlink fix to avoid the 2GB-bundle leak), set `spineSrc`/`spineWidth/Height`/`footageGrade`/`overlayClips` (caption track ‚Üí WordCue[] in frames; overlay/broll tracks ‚Üí `fromF/toF`+asset). Persist the props sidecar.
5. **Remotion renders overlay-over-footage** ‚Äî full-frame `<OffthreadVideo src={staticFile(spineSrc)} muted>` base (BrollBackground:699 mechanism), wrapped in `GradePipeline`/`url(#grade)` for color **on the footage**, then the existing `Karaoke`/`SubtitleLayer` + b-roll/overlay/mograph `<Sequence>`s. One H.264 render, audio ignored.
6. **ffmpeg builds the audio mix separately** ‚Äî per audio-clip extract‚Üí`buildAudioFiltergraph`‚Üí`adelay`‚Üí`amix`; `addMusicBed` duck (word-accurate `buildDuckSpans`); `masterAudio` to `mix.loudnessTarget`.
7. **ffmpeg muxes** video+audio, stream-copying the Remotion video (no re-encode).

**Why, and what's rejected.** This is the **same division of labour as today's shorts** (Remotion owns pixels, ffmpeg owns mastered audio) and reuses both halves' existing builders. **Rejected: "ffmpeg burns overlays"** (loses Remotion's caption/mograph engine). **Rejected: "alpha-PNG overlay sequence muxed by ffmpeg"** (double video encode, grade can't see the footage, color-space mismatch). The OffthreadVideo-base path means **grade.tsx grades real pixels for free** and there is **one render path** (HybridPost is a superset of Post ‚Äî `spineSrc` absent ‚áí byte-identical to today's generated render).

**Two qualifications, both opinionated:** (a) **Chapter the spine for anything over ~3‚Äì4 min** ‚Äî OffthreadVideo seeking across one long file is the real perf risk; reuse the longform render-then-concat precedent (one HybridPost per chapter ‚Üí concat finals). (b) **Footage gets `GradePipeline` primary only by default**; the heavy generative look-layers (`ColorGrade` wash, `CinematicTreatment`) are gated behind a flag for ingested runs ‚Äî they over-darken real footage.

M1 (timeline schema) is **done**, which unblocks N1‚ÄìN4. N5 leans on the NLE trims (**M10 compileTimeline, M11 trim/ripple/J-L**) + the craft passes (M2‚ÄìM5 color done, M6‚ÄìM9 audio done). N6 is the deepest and depends on M10/M11 + N1‚ÄìN4.

| **N5.3** | Soli editor persona presents `EditPlan` as content-aware analysis + edit-proposal gate; per-thread autonomy opt-in; `creative_edit_plan_{get,approve,reject}` + role-matrix gating | N5.1 | **M / 3d** | guided ‚Üî autonomous chat editing with the approval gate |
| **N5.4** | `learnFootageTaste` in `perf.ts` (reel-length band, cut cadence, trim aggressiveness from `reviewCut`); router reads it | N5.3 | **S / 2d** | "tighten to 60s" starts from learned cadence next time |

**Interleave with the program.** Phase A (parallel to color/audio work, after M1): **N1 ‚Üí N2 ‚Üí N3a/N4a** ‚Äî ships import + understand + a seeded timeline + a caption track (preview-only until N6). Phase B: **M10 (compileTimeline) + M11 (trims)** land the NLE ‚Äî then **N6.0‚ÄìN6.2** prove the hybrid render and **N4b** lights up caption render. Phase C: **N5.0** (router/montage analysis can be built+tested first against a hand-seeded `understanding` fixture) ‚Üí **N5.1‚ÄìN5.4** + **N6.3/N6.4** complete the Odysser loop. Critical path = M10/M11 ‚Üí N6 ‚Üí N5.1; everything else parallelizes.

### 7.1.6 Risks & open decisions

**Risks (with mitigations):**
- **Rotation/VFR/fps truthfulness (N1).** Read rotation from `side_data_list` ‚à™ `tags.rotate` ‚à™ display-matrix and **bake it in the transcode**, or N3 frame math + the Remotion overlay are 90¬∞ off. **Keep source fps** (24/25/29.97/60) ‚Äî do **NOT** copy `render.ts`'s `-r 30`. CFR-normalize VFR or caption timing drifts.
- **The Whisper segment edit is load-bearing (N2).** `whisper-words.py` currently flattens to words and drops segment boundaries; without segments, speaker-turn + redundancy have no structure. Reuse **`editor-tools.transcribeVideoAudio`** (16k-mono + words already), do not add a third Whisper caller.
- **No render path for an ingested transcript until N6.** Captions are recomputed from voice-synth today (`run.ts`/`rerender.ts`); `auto_subtitle` produces a track that **won't render until N6 reads `Mix.subtitles.source="track"`**. Ship N4a as preview-only or land it with N6.1.
- **Sec‚Üîframe drift.** Understanding stores seconds; Karaoke needs frames. Use `timeline.ts toFrames` **round** (not floor) at the single caption-build boundary or word highlights desync from the cut.
- **Color-space double-grade (N6).** Normalize every cut part to `yuv420p`/`bt709`, and tune grade against **measured** scopes (`editor_color_scopes`), not prose.
- **OffthreadVideo seek cost / spine in public/ (N6).** Chapter spines >3‚Äì4 min; **symlink** the spine into `public/` (reuse the broll symlink fix) ‚Äî never copy a large mp4 in.
- **Source audio ‚âÝ synth voice (N3/N6).** A1 is production audio (voice+bed mixed); the duck/voiceSrc machinery built for a clean synth stem doesn't apply cleanly ‚Äî `buildFootageAudio` must treat source audio accordingly. **Gate `speed‚âÝ1` off source-audio clips in v1** (atempo-match later) or A/V drifts.
- **Idempotent re-seed by shot id, not array index (N3)** ‚Äî or a re-understand wipes the user's manual cut.
- **Three projections risk (N5).** Resolved by decision: for ingested runs the **timeline is the sole write path**; the Edl is unused.
- **Autonomy bypassing the human (N5).** `creative_apply_plan`'s approve path is **withheld from autonomous callers** unless the run is explicitly flagged; never auto-cross the publish gate.
- **Re-render cost (N5/N6).** Batch multiple EditOps into **one** compile+render; support preview-quality re-render; cap iterations like `loop.ts`.
- **PII (CLAUDE.md absolute rule).** `source.originalPath` may carry a home-dir path/filename ‚Äî keep it in private `data/runs` JSON, serve only via the ALLOWED_DIRS gate, never echo absolute paths into anything publishable. `importVideo` must `saveItem(item, ctx)` for workspace scoping.

**Open decisions for the human:**
1. **Hybrid render architecture sign-off** ‚Äî recommend the OffthreadVideo-base path above (one encode, grade sees footage, one render path). Couples render time to OffthreadVideo seek perf ‚Üí chapter long spines.
2. **Auto-chain ingest ‚Üí understand?** Recommend **default-OFF** (probe-only import is instant), with an `--understand` flag to opt in.
3. **Long-form spine:** chapter-and-concat over ~3‚Äì4 min (recommended) vs one render.
4. **Keep the user's original after normalize?** Recommend keep (re-grade fidelity for N6 / future higher-quality re-transcode), with an opt-out `--no-keep-original`.
5. **Montage destination:** clone a new ContentItem for "re-montage as a different cut" (non-destructive), mutate in place for "tighten this".
6. **Caption delivery:** burned-in (the look) **and** emit an SRT sidecar from the same word cues (accessibility/platform-native) ‚Äî cheap, both from one transcript.
7. **Diarization depth:** ship the pause+energy heuristic v1, schema-stable for a later pyannote/MLX swap.
8. **Source-clip grade/mix ‚Üî M2‚ÄìM9:** does `compileTimeline` synthesize a Scene per clip the grade/mix attaches to, or add clip-level grade/mix fields? Needs a contract with the M10 compiler + colorist/mixer owners.
9. **Hosted caps:** max source duration/size for the non-self-host path (self-host uncapped) ‚Äî a config knob to bound transcode cost.
10. **sha256 dedup short-circuit** on re-import ‚Äî useful but adds a content index; defer unless cheap.

---

Synthesis complete. The plan above is ready to append to `docs/DAVINCI-ROADMAP.md` under the existing ¬ß7 stub (replacing its closing "To be planned in detail by a dedicated architect workflow." line). Every file/line reference was verified against the working tree. Net new surface area: 1 schema block set (`SourceVideo`/`Understanding`/`EditPlan` families, all additive-optional), ~6 engine modules (`ingest.ts`, `seed-from-footage.ts`, `auto-subtitle.ts`, `edit-router.ts`, `apply-plan.ts`, `montage.ts`), `renderHybrid`+`HybridPost.tsx`, one `whisper-words.py` edit (emit segments), and ~18 registry tools ‚Äî against ~70% reuse of existing ffprobe/Whisper/videoDiagnostics/perception/passes/grade/audio/render machinery.
